name: BMI Calculator
titleTag: BMI Calculator - Calculate Body Mass Index | Smart Calculator Pro
metaDescription: Calculate your BMI (Body Mass Index) with our free BMI calculator. Understand your weight category and health implications.
intro: Calculate your Body Mass Index (BMI) to understand if your weight is in a healthy range for your height.
shortDescription: Calculate Body Mass Index from height and weight.
longDescription: Body Mass Index (BMI) is a simple calculation using a person's height and weight. This calculator helps you determine if your weight is within a healthy range.
problemSolved: Helps users understand their weight category and associated health risks.

formula.label: BMI Formula
formula.plainText: BMI equals weight in pounds times 703, divided by height in inches squared.

formulaExplanation: BMI uses a mathematical formula to calculate weight status
The formula was developed in the 19th century by Adolphe Quetelet
703 is the conversion factor for metric to imperial units
methodologyNotes: BMI is a general screening tool and does not measure body fat directly
BMI may overestimate body fat in athletes and underestimate it in older adults
Always consult a healthcare provider for personalized health advice

example.scenario: An adult who is 68 inches tall and weighs 150 pounds.
example.resultSummary: Their BMI is approximately 22.8, which falls within the Normal weight range.

resultInterpretation.defaultText: Your BMI result places you in one of four weight categories. While BMI is a useful screening tool, it doesn't account for muscle mass, bone density, or overall body composition.

thresholds.label: Underweight
thresholds.interpretation: BMI below 18.5 suggests underweight. Consider consulting a healthcare provider.

thresholds.label: Normal weight
thresholds.interpretation: BMI between 18.5 and 24.9 is considered a healthy weight range.

thresholds.label: Overweight
thresholds.interpretation: BMI between 25 and 29.9 suggests overweight. Lifestyle changes may be beneficial.

thresholds.label: Obese
thresholds.interpretation: BMI of 30 or higher indicates obesity. Consult a healthcare provider.

warningMessages: BMI is not suitable for children, pregnant women, or highly muscular individuals.
A healthy BMI doesn't guarantee good health—other factors like diet, exercise, and genetics matter.

faq.question: What is BMI?
faq.answer: BMI (Body Mass Index) is a measure of body fat based on height and weight. It's used as a screening tool to identify possible weight problems in adults.

faq.question: What is a healthy BMI?
faq.answer: A healthy BMI for adults is typically between 18.5 and 24.9. However, individual factors like muscle mass, age, and overall health should also be considered.

faq.question: Does BMI work for everyone?
faq.answer: No. BMI may not be accurate for athletes, older adults, pregnant women, or people with high muscle mass. It's a general screening tool, not a diagnostic tool.

disclaimer: This calculator provides general information only and is not a substitute for professional medical advice. Consult a healthcare provider for personalized guidance.
